task: Expand shape vocabulary and add Raven-authentic visual elements (diamond, pentagon, hexagon, star, cross, arrow + hatching/dots/cross-hatch fill patterns)

notes.message: Current shapes: circle, square, triangle (3). Real Raven matrices use ~9 shapes and overlay patterns like hatching/dots. Plan: (1) Add 6 new shapes: diamond, pentagon, hexagon, star, cross, arrow. (2) Add a new 'fillPattern' attribute with values: solid, hatched, dotted, crossHatch — drawn via SVG <pattern> defs clipped to the shape. (3) Increase innerLine to include 'cross' (both H+V). This gives us 9 shapes × 4 fills × 3 colors × 3 sizes × 4 inner-lines × 3 rotations × 3 counts = massive combinatorial space.

Architecture: shapes are pure SVG path functions — no images needed. fillPattern will use SVG <defs><pattern> elements rendered once per cell, with the shape using fill='url(#patternId)'. The pattern tiles lines/dots/crosshatch. Config stays flexible: default puzzle-config.json keeps only 3 shapes enabled to preserve the 3×3 Latin square, but users can pick any subset of 3+ shapes for their grid size.

Implementation complete. (1) types.ts: ShapeType expanded to 9 (circle/square/triangle/diamond/pentagon/hexagon/star/cross/arrow), InnerLineType gained 'cross', added FillPatternType (solid/hatched/dotted/crossHatch), CellData+AttributeName extended. (2) config.ts: shape defaults now list all 9, fillPattern added (disabled), enabledAttributes knows about fillPattern. (3) puzzle-config.json: mirrors config.ts. (4) generator.ts: ALL_ATTR_NAMES+cellKey include fillPattern. (5) ShapeCell.tsx: all shapes via SVG path builders, SVG <pattern>+<clipPath> defs for fill textures, cross inner-line, renderSingleShape composes everything. tsc+vite build clean.
